  "nearby_text": "The Wishart is the sampling distribution for covariance matrices (just like the $ \\chi^{2} $ is associated with the distribution of a sample variance for data drawn from a normal; Chapter 11). The pdf of the Wishart distribution is Recall that the trace (tr) of a matrix is just the sum of its diagonal elements, $ \\mathrm{tr}(\\mathbf{A}) = \\sum A_{ii} $ (Appendix 5). Odell and Feiveson (1966) presented an algorithm for generating random draws from the Wishart."
}

label: Formula A2.92
nearby_text: If $ \mathbf{Z} \sim \mathbf{W}_n(\mathbf{V}) $, then $ \mathbf{Z}^{-1} \sim \mathbf{W}_n^{-1}(\mathbf{V}^{-1}) $, where $ \mathbf{W}^{-1} $ denotes the inverse-Wishart distribution. The density function for an inverse-Wishart distributed random matrix, $ \mathbf{W} $, is which is the distribution of the inverse of the sample covariance matrix.

        "latex": "t_{n-1}=\\left(\\frac{\\overline{x}-\\mu}{\\sigma/\\sqrt{n}}\\right)\\left(\\frac{1}{\\sqrt{\\mathrm{Var}(x)/\\sigma^{2}}}\\right)=\\frac{U}{\\sqrt{\\chi_{n-1}^{2}/(n-1)}}",
        "label": "Formula A2.73",
        "section": "POSTERIOR DISTRIBUTIONS UNDER NORMALITY ASSUMPTIONS",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.74",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.75",
        "latex": "t_{\\nu}(\\mu,\\sigma)=\\mu+\\sigma\\cdot t_{\\nu}",
        "label": "Formula A2.75",
        "section": "POSTERIOR DISTRIBUTIONS UNDER NORMALITY ASSUMPTIONS",
        "subsection": "Student's t Distribution",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.76",
        "latex": "E[t_{\\nu}(\\mu,\\sigma)]=\\mu\\quad and\\quad\\sigma^{2}[t_{\\nu}(\\mu,\\sigma)]=\\sigma^{2}\\frac{\\nu}{\\nu-2}\\quad for\\nu>2",
        "label": "Formula A2.76",
        "section": "POSTERIOR DISTRIBUTIONS UNDER NORMALITY ASSUMPTIONS",
        "subsection": "Student's t Distribution",
        "position": 75,

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.77",
        "latex": "p(x\\mid\\nu,\\mu,\\sigma)=\\frac{\\Gamma([\\nu+1]/2)}{\\Gamma(\\nu/2)\\sigma\\sqrt{\\pi\\nu}}\\left[1+\\frac{1}{\\nu}\\left(\\frac{x-\\mu}{\\sigma}\\right)^{2}\\right]^{-(\\nu+1)/2}",
        "label": "Formula A2.77",

      {

        "label": "Formula A2.78",

      {
        "id": "formula_A2.79",
        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}\\sim\\chi_{\\nu_{0},\\sigma_{0}^{2}}^{-2}\\qquad\\mathrm{a n d}\\qquad\\mu\\mid\\sigma^{2}\\sim\\mathrm{N}\\left(\\mu_{0},\\frac{\\sigma^{2}}{\\kappa_{0}}\\right)",
        "label": "Formula A2.79",

      {

        "latex": "\\sigma^{2}\\left|\\textbf{x}\\sim\\chi_{\\nu_{n},\\sigma_{n}^{2}}^{-2}\\right.\\quad\\mathrm{a n d}\\quad\\mu\\left|\\textbf{x}\\sim\\textbf{t}_{\\nu_{n}}\\left(\\mu_{n},\\frac{\\sigma_{n}^{2}}{\\kappa_{n}}\\right)\\right.",
        "label": "Formula A2.80",

      {
        "id": "formula_A2.81",
        "latex": "\\nu_{n}=\\nu_{0}+n,\\qquad\\kappa_{n}=\\kappa_{0}+n",

      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.82",
        "latex": "\\mu_{n}=\\mu_{0}\\frac{\\kappa_{0}}{\\kappa_{n}}+\\overline{x}\\frac{n}{\\kappa_{n}}=\\mu_{0}\\frac{\\kappa_{0}}{\\kappa_{0}+n}+\\overline{x}\\frac{n}{\\kappa_{0}+n}",
        "label": "Formula A2.82",

      {

        "latex": "p(n_{1},\\cdots n_{k})=\\frac{n!}{n_{1}!n_{2}!\\cdots n_{k}!}\\ p_{1}^{n_{1}}\\cdots p_{k}^{n_{k}}\\quad where\\quad\\sum_{i}n_{i}=n\\quad and\\quad\\sum_{i}p_{i}=1",

      {
        "id": "formula_A2.85",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}p(x_1,\\cdots x_k\\mid\\alpha_1,\\cdots,\\alpha_k)=\\frac{\\Gamma(\\alpha_0)}{\\Gamma(\\alpha_1)\\cdots\\Gamma(\\alpha_k)}x_1^{\\alpha_1-1}\\cdots x_k^{\\alpha_k-1}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A2.85",
        "section": "CONJUGATE PRIORS",
        "subsection": "The Beta and Dirichlet Distributions",
        "position": 84,
        "symbols_defined": [

          "p",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.86",
        "latex": "\\alpha_{0}=\\sum_{i=1}^{k}\\alpha_{i}\\quad with\\quad\\alpha_{i}>0,\\quad and\\quad\\sum_{i=1}^{k}x_{i}=1\\quad with\\quad0\\leq x_{i}\\leq1",
        "label": "Formula A2.86",
        "section": "CONJUGATE PRIORS",
        "subsection": "The Beta and Dirichlet Distributions",

        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.87",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\mu_{x_i}={\\alpha_i\\over\\alpha_0},\\quad\\sigma^2(x_i)={\\alpha_i(\\alpha_0-\\alpha_i)\\over\\alpha_0^2(\\alpha_0+1)},\\quad{\\rm and}\\quad\\sigma(x_i,x_j)=-{\\alpha_i\\alpha_j\\over\\alpha_0^2(\\alpha_0+1)}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A2.87",
        "section": "CONJUGATE PRIORS",
        "subsection": "The Beta and Dirichlet Distributions",
        "position": 86,
        "symbols_defined": [

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.88",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}p(x)=\\frac{\\Gamma(\\alpha+\\beta)}{\\Gamma(\\alpha)\\Gamma(\\beta)}\\; x^{\\alpha-1}(1-x)^{\\beta-1}\\quad{\\rm for}\\quad0\\le x\\le1,\\quad\\alpha,\\;\\beta>0\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A2.88",
        "section": "CONJUGATE PRIORS",
        "subsection": "The Beta and Dirichlet Distributions",

        ]
      },
      {
        "id": "formula_A2.89",
        "latex": "\\begin{align*}\\mu=\\frac{\\alpha}{\\alpha+\\beta}\\qquad{\\rm and}\\qquad\\sigma^2=\\frac{\\alpha\\beta}{(\\alpha+\\beta)^2(\\alpha+\\beta-1)}\\end{align*}",
        "label": "Formula A2.89",

      {
        "id": "formula_A2.90",
        "latex": "\\mathbf{W}=\\sum_{i=1}^{n}\\mathbf{x}_{i}\\mathbf{x}_{i}^{T}\\sim W_{n}(\\mathbf{V})",

      },
      {

        "latex": "p(\\mathbf{W}\\mid\\mathbf{V})=2^{-n k/2}\\pi^{-k(k-1)/k}\\mid\\mathbf{V}\\mid^{-n/2}\\mid\\mathbf{W}\\mid^{(n+k+1)/2}\\frac{\\exp\\left(-\\frac{1}{2}\\mathbf{t}\\mathbf{r}\\left[\\mathbf{V}^{-1}\\mathbf{W}\\right]\\right)}{\\prod_{i=1}^{k}\\Gamma\\left(\\frac{n+1-i}{2}\\right)}",
        "label": "Formula A2.91",
        "section": "CONJUGATE PRIORS",
        "subsection": "Wishart and Inverse-Wishart Distributions",

  },
  "nearby_text": "If $ \\mathbf{Z} \\sim \\mathbf{W}_n(\\mathbf{V}) $, then $ \\mathbf{Z}^{-1} \\sim \\mathbf{W}_n^{-1}(\\mathbf{V}^{-1}) $, where $ \\mathbf{W}^{-1} $ denotes the inverse-Wishart distribution. The density function for an inverse-Wishart distributed random matrix, $ \\mathbf{W} $, is which is the distribution of the inverse of the sample covariance matrix."
}
